scenario: The package and plugin artifact include the docs, VISION contract, scorecards, runtime entries, MCP server, and declared loo_* tools needed by a local beta user.
expected_public_safe_evidence: published-beta tarball lookup, global fallback install, OpenClaw install fallback, and dogfood fallback commands
published package smoke report packagePathOk and setupRequired classifications
published package smoke report setupRecovery classification, requiredSetup, readinessProof, and next safe commands
gateway setup classification from sanitized tool-smoke evidence
optional configured-profile gateway proof kept separate from fresh published profile readiness
clean OpenClaw profile name for published-beta proof
gateway setup guidance for credential/device-pairing blockers
OpenClaw dogfood installOutcome.recognizedMarker when a known OpenClaw output marker is detected
clean-profile linked install marker or already-installed diagnostic
pass_criteria: Package includes README.md, VISION.md, docs, evals, plugin manifest, and compiled runtime entries.
Plugin manifest declares the loo_ prefix and expected read/control/admin tools.

Onboarding status names the published beta package, clean profile, npm registry check, tarball lookup, global install, guarded tarball fallback install, OpenClaw install, OpenClaw tarball fallback install, dogfood, dogfood tarball fallback, and tool-smoke recovery commands.
Onboarding status classifies the local package version against the npm beta dist-tag and records gateway setup status without raw npm or OpenClaw output.
Published package smoke report combines sanitized npm beta, dogfood, fresh-profile tool-smoke, and optional configured-profile tool-smoke evidence without storing raw command output.

Published package smoke report classifies clean-profile setup recovery as ready, credential_required, device_pairing_required, scope_upgrade_required, token_rotation_required, setup_required, or package_failure_or_unknown.
Credential-required setup recovery includes deterministic token generation, env-ref onboarding, gateway status, and fresh-profile tool-smoke commands using placeholders or env refs only.
Configured-profile gateway proof is separately named and never marks the fresh published profile ready; setupRequired and publishedSmokeReady remain driven only by the fresh-profile toolSmoke block.
OpenClaw dogfood distinguishes installed, already_installed, link_force_unsupported, and failed linked-install outcomes without raw local paths.
Known OpenClaw prose markers are reduced to public-safe installOutcome.recognizedMarker ids instead of copying raw command output.
Release preflight/status fail closed on missing approvals without performing publish or release actions.
fail_criteria: Package omits VISION.md, scorecards, OpenClaw manifest, or MCP runtime entry.
Tool declarations drift from the MCP registry.

Onboarding status lacks a clean-profile published-beta recovery path, omits guarded npm selector drift tarball fallback commands for global/OpenClaw/dogfood paths, or presents setup blockers as product defects.
Post-install self-check accepts raw gateway/npm output, omits version mismatch status, or hides setup-required classification.
Published package smoke report treats gateway setup-required as a package defect, hides package version mismatch, or masks fresh-profile setup-required status with configured-profile proof.

Published package smoke report lacks setupRecovery, omits next safe commands for credential/device/scope/token blockers, omits deterministic credential setup commands, stores raw tokens, or reports readiness without fresh-profile tool-smoke proof.
Linked install failures are reported as generic failures when the plugin is already loaded, or raw OpenClaw stdout/stderr enters public evidence.
Release commands perform npm publish, GitHub Release creation, live control, or GUI mutation.
known_gaps: The first npm install selector attempt can hit npm selector cutoff drift even when registry metadata exposes the beta; onboarding now exposes guarded registry tarball lookup plus fallback install commands for global CLI, OpenClaw plugin install, and dogfood paths.

Fresh OpenClaw profiles still need scoped gateway credentials or env-ref onboarding before tool invocation; #298 proves that setup path can reach ready, and missing setup must remain classified as setup-required rather than package failure.

Fresh OpenClaw gateway setup recovery is classified in `published-smoke` as public-safe setupRecovery states with deterministic credential setup commands; ready is claimable only when fresh-profile tool-smoke is ready.
Configured local gateway proof can show the agent workflow works on an already provisioned profile, but clean-profile readiness must still be driven by fresh-profile tool-smoke evidence.

This scorecard points at #298 fresh-profile gateway-ready evidence for the protocol-4 backend caller fix; it does not prove stable release, npm latest promotion, live control, GUI mutation, or enterprise/customer readiness.
next_action: Publish beta.35 after PR/CI/review gates so the public CLI contains the protocol-4 backend caller fix, then use the #298 packet as one input to a separate stable/latest release decision.
proof_boundary: This proves current beta install/load evidence plus public-safe fresh-profile gateway-ready proof for the protocol-4 backend caller path; it does not prove stable release, npm latest promotion, live control, GUI mutation, or enterprise/customer readiness.
